name: Using Galaxy and Managing your Data / Tutorial List
description: A collection of microtutorials explaining various features of the Galaxy user interface and how to manipulate data within Galaxy. It provides guidance on using Galaxy effectively. These tutorials cover fundamental aspects of data handling and analysis within the Galaxy environment.
teaches: Data uploading and downloading in Galaxy

name: Plants
description: A collection of tutorials utilizing plant datasets for analysis. Focuses on various topics within plant biology and bioinformatics. Provides learning resources for those interested in analyzing plant-related data using Galaxy. This topic covers a range of tutorials designed to build skills in plant data analysis.

name: Statistics and machine learning
description: This learning pathway provides tutorials on statistical analyses for omics data and machine learning techniques using Galaxy tools. It covers a range of topics within statistics and machine learning applicable to biological data analysis. It’s a curated list of tutorials for learning these skills.

name: Visualisation / Tutorial List
description: This page serves as a central hub for training materials related to data visualisation within the Galaxy platform. It provides access to various tutorials and resources for learning visualisation techniques. Includes links to FAQs and discussion forums. The materials cover a range of skills, from basic chart creation to advanced data exploration.

name: ELIXIR / Tutorial List
description: Tutorials developed within ELIXIR, a European life sciences infrastructure, bringing together scientists from over 240 research institutes spread over 21 Member countries. ELIXIR is partly funded by the European Commission within the Research Infrastructures programme of Horizon 2020.  These tutorials cover a wide range of bioinformatics topics, and are often used in conjunction with the Galaxy platform. They are aimed at researchers, students, and anyone interested in learning more about bioinformatics.

name: Epigenetics / Tutorial List
description: This learning pathway covers epigenetics, specifically DNA methylation as a mechanism involved in gene expression, X-Chromosome inactivation, imprinting, and silencing of repetitive elements. It provides access to tutorials on the topic.
teaches: Explain the role of DNA methylation in gene expression
Explain the silencing of repetitive elements

name: Metabolomics / Tutorial List
description: Training material to analyse Mass spectrometry data in Galaxy. Covers Metabolomics (LCMS, FIAMS, GCMS, NMR) and imaging techniques, providing resources for data analysis within the Galaxy platform.
teaches: Analysis of metabolomics data using Galaxy
Interpretation of LCMS, FIAMS, GCMS, and NMR data

name: Proteomics / Tutorial List
description: Training material for proteomics workflows in Galaxy. Focuses on tutorials related to proteomics analysis. It's part of the Galaxy Training Network and aims to provide resources for learning these workflows. Includes FAQs and links to Galaxy Help forum.

name: Single Cell / Tutorial List
description: This page provides training materials and practicals for single-cell analysis, with a particular focus on scRNA-seq. It's a curated list of tutorials related to this topic. It links to FAQs, help forums, and allows contribution via GitHub.  The materials cover a range of techniques and analysis methods for single-cell data. This resource aims to support both beginners and experienced researchers in the field.
teaches: Apply bioinformatics tools to single-cell data

name: Synthetic Biology / Tutorial List
description: Synthetic Biology is the engineering of biology – the deliberate (re)design and construction of novel biological and biologically based parts, devices and systems to perform new functions for useful purposes, that draws on principles elucidated from biology and engineering. By applying these principles to living systems, Synthetic Biology overcomes mimicry and optimisation-led research and introduces a rationale and systematic approach to the construction and (re)design. Synthetic Biology is at the intersection of engineering, bioscience, chemistry, and information technology. The goal of synthetic biology is to extend or modify the behavior of organisms and engineer them to perform new tasks. In this section, the tutorials show how to design metabolic pathways for producing the desired chemical targets.
teaches: Application of engineering principles to biology

name: Transcriptomics / Tutorial List
description: Training material for all kinds of transcriptomics analysis. Includes links to tutorials and resources for learning about the topic. Focuses on using Galaxy for analysis. This resource provides comprehensive guides for both beginners and those with some experience in the field, covering key concepts and practical applications.

name: Variant Analysis / Tutorial List
description: Genetic differences (variants) between healthy and diseased tissue, between individuals of a population, or between strains of an organism can provide mechanistic insight into disease processes and the natural function of affected genes. The tutorials in this section show how to detect evidence for genetic variants in next-generation sequencing data, a process termed variant calling. Of equal importance, they also demonstrate how you can interpret, for a range of different organisms, the resulting sets of variants by predicting their molecular effects on genes and proteins, by annotating previously observed variants with published knowledge, and by trying to link phenotypes of the sequenced samples to their variant genotypes.
teaches: Detecting genetic variants in next-generation sequencing data
Predicting molecular effects of variants on genes and proteins

name: Galaxy Server administration / Tutorial List
description: Here you can learn everything you need to become a Pro Galaxy Administrator! These resources cover everything from setup, to configuration, to monitoring, to customisation. This collection of tutorials is designed for those looking to manage and maintain their own Galaxy servers.
teaches: User and security management for Galaxy Servers

name: Development in Galaxy / Tutorial List
description: Galaxy is an open-source project. Everyone can contribute to its development with core Galaxy development, integration of softwares in Galaxy environment, and more. These tutorials provide resources for developers interested in extending and improving Galaxy's functionality.

name: Contributing to the Galaxy Training Material / Tutorial List
description: Galaxy is a powerful platform for bioinformatics training, offering access to over 7,000 tools and catering to users without extensive computer science backgrounds. The Galaxy Training Network established a new infrastructure in 2016 to deliver open and online Galaxy training materials, driven by community effort.  This resource emphasizes technology usage, outlines available resources, and promotes accessibility for researchers.  It is a scalable solution for delivering bioinformatics education.

name: Teaching and Hosting Galaxy training / Tutorial List
description: Galaxy is a powerful platform for bioinformatics training, offering access to a vast collection of tools (almost 5,000 in the ToolShed) and catering to users without extensive computer science backgrounds. It equips users with practical technology skills, highlights available resources, and fosters a scalable learning environment. The Galaxy Training Network supports instructors and workshop organizers with tutorials and regular discussion meetings.
teaches: Using the Galaxy platform for bioinformatics analysis
Designing and hosting Galaxy training workshops
Workflow creation and management in Galaxy

name: Hall of Fame
description: This page lists and thanks contributors to the Galaxy training materials. It’s a collection acknowledging individuals involved in developing and maintaining tutorials. It currently highlights 525 contributors.

name: Galaxy Training Academy 2026
description: The Galaxy Training Academy is a self-paced online training event for beginners and advanced learners who want to improve their data analysis skills in Galaxy and/or in popular fields in bioinformatics. Over the course of one week, we offer a diverse selection of learning tracks for you.

name: Galaxy Beyond Basics: Mastering Workflows, Automation, and Scalability
description: Join us for an intensive, week-long, in-person training designed to elevate your Galaxy expertise to new heights. This workshop is tailored for data scientists, advanced Galaxy users, and team leaders who need to scale, automate, and publish their data analysis workflows for batch processing and production-level applications.

name: GTN Contributor: Common Infrastructure for National Cohorts in Europe, Canada, and Africa
description: This page describes the CINECA project, recognized as a contributor by the Galaxy Training Network. It's part of the Hall of Fame, highlighting projects supporting the Galaxy community. The page doesn't detail the project's specifics but frames it within the context of a wider infrastructure. It is a collection of tutorials developed and maintained by the Galaxy community.
teaches: Understanding the role of CINECA in the Galaxy Training Network
Overview of infrastructure for national cohorts
The benefits of contributing to open-source scientific projects

name: GTN Contributor: CORBEL
description: This page describes CORBEL as a contributor to the Galaxy Training Network. It’s a collection of tutorials developed and maintained by the Galaxy community. teaches: Understanding the role of CORBEL in Galaxy training.

author.name: Ministry of Science, Research and Arts

name: GTN Contributor: National Center for Biotechnology Information
description: This page acknowledges the National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI) as a contributor to the Galaxy Training Network. It's a recognition of their support for the development and maintenance of tutorials by the worldwide Galaxy community. This contribution helps ensure the availability of high-quality bioinformatics training resources.
teaches: Understanding the role of NCBI in bioinformatics.
Appreciating community contributions to open-source training materials.

name: GTN Contributor: National Institutes of Health
description: This page recognizes the National Institutes of Health (NIH) as a valued contributor to the Galaxy Training Network (GTN). The GTN is a collaborative effort delivering accessible, high-quality training materials for data analysis.  NIH contributions support a wide range of tutorials developed and maintained by the Galaxy community, serving as a central resource for NIH-related bioinformatics training. This highlights the NIH’s commitment to fostering data science skills.

name: GTN Contributor: NL-BioImaging
description: A listing recognizing NL-BioImaging as a contributor to the Galaxy Training Network. It highlights a collection of tutorials developed and maintained by the worldwide Galaxy community. This page serves as a thank you and acknowledgement of their contributions.

name: GTN Contributor: National Science Foundation
description: This page acknowledges the National Science Foundation as a contributor to Galaxy Training Network (GTN). It’s a collection of tutorials developed and maintained by the worldwide Galaxy community.  The GTN provides accessible, open, and reproducible training materials for the Galaxy platform and related tools.  This contributor page recognizes NSF's support in making this training possible. It highlights the impact of their funding on the bioinformatics community.

name: GTN Contributor: The Open University
description: This page acknowledges The Open University as a contributor to the Galaxy Training Network. It’s a Hall of Fame entry showcasing their involvement, likely through tutorial development. It’s a collection of tutorials developed and maintained by the worldwide Galaxy community, providing resources for data science and bioinformatics skills.

name: GTN Contributor: Università Vita-Salute San Raffaele
description: This page profiles Università Vita-Salute San Raffaele as a contributor to the Galaxy Training Network. It's a listing of tutorials developed and maintained by them, part of the wider Galaxy community effort. The page itself doesn’t contain training material, but points to the contributor’s materials. It serves as a directory for their contributions.

name: GTN Contributor: Sciensano
description: This page acknowledges Sciensano as a contributor to the Galaxy Training Network. It’s a collection of tutorials developed and maintained by the worldwide Galaxy community. Sciensano’s contributions are vital to the GTN’s mission of providing high-quality, accessible bioinformatics training. This page serves as public recognition of their dedication and support.
teaches: The importance of community contribution to open-source bioinformatics training.
How to recognize key contributors to the Galaxy Training Network.

name: GTN Contributor: Sorbonne Université
description: This page highlights Sorbonne Université as a contributor to the Galaxy Training Network. It showcases their contributions to the development and maintenance of tutorials within the worldwide Galaxy community. The page serves as recognition for their dedication to bioinformatics training and open science practices.  It's a valuable resource for identifying key contributors to the GTN ecosystem.
teaches: Contribution to open-source bioinformatics training materials

name: GTN Contributor: 🖖🏾Single-cell & sPatial Omics Community of practice
description: A collection of tutorials developed and maintained by the worldwide Galaxy community focused on single-cell and spatial omics. It appears to be a curated resource showcasing work from a specific community of practice. This resource provides learning materials for analyzing single-cell and spatial omics data using the Galaxy platform. It caters to researchers and bioinformaticians interested in these cutting-edge technologies. The community aspect suggests collaborative learning and knowledge sharing.

name: GTN Contributor: Swiss Tropical and Public Health Institute
description: Acknowledges Swiss Tropical and Public Health Institute as a contributor to Galaxy Training Network. The GTN is a collection of tutorials developed and maintained by the worldwide Galaxy community, providing training in bioinformatics and data analysis.

author.name: Swiss Tropical and Public Health Institute

name: GTN Contributor: SURF
description: This page highlights SURF's contributions to the Galaxy Training Network (GTN). The GTN provides a collection of tutorials developed and maintained by the worldwide Galaxy community.  Being a "Hall of Fame" entry indicates high quality or significant impact of these resources. SURF is recognized for its valuable contributions to the training materials.
teaches: Using the Galaxy platform for data analysis

name: GTN Contributor: TB-CAPT
description: This page recognizes TB-CAPT as a contributor to the Galaxy Training Network (GTN). GTN provides a collection of tutorials developed and maintained by the worldwide Galaxy community. It serves as a hall of fame entry highlighting contributions to bioinformatics training.

name: GTN Contributor: UFZ - Helmholtz-Zentrum für Umweltforschung UFZ
description: A collection of tutorials developed and maintained by the worldwide Galaxy community, contributed by UFZ. This page acts as a landing point for materials created by this organization. It’s part of the Galaxy Training Network’s Hall of Fame, showcasing their contributions to bioinformatics training.
teaches: Using the Galaxy platform for data analysis

name: GTN Contributor: Université Grenoble-Alpes
description: This page recognizes Université Grenoble-Alpes as a contributor to the Galaxy Training Network. It's a listing of tutorials and materials developed and maintained by the worldwide Galaxy community. It’s a recognition of contributions, not a tutorial *per se*. This highlights their commitment to open-source science and training in data analysis.

name: GTN Contributor: UK Medical Research Council
description: This page acknowledges the UK Medical Research Council as a contributor to the Galaxy Training Network. It highlights a collection of tutorials developed and maintained by the Galaxy community. This resource serves as recognition for their support of bioinformatics training and resources. The page links to the broader Galaxy Training Material repository.
teaches: The importance of community contributions to scientific training.

name: Hall of Fame
description: This page is a collection of particularly useful Galaxy Training materials, curated by the Galaxy Training Network. It serves as a starting point for finding high-quality tutorials. The materials are developed and maintained by the global Galaxy community. It acts as a directory to high-value training resources.

name: Learning Pathways
description: Learning pathways are sets of tutorials curated for you by community experts to form a coherent set of lessons around a topic, building up knowledge as you go. We always recommend to follow the tutorials in the order they are listed in the pathway.
teaches: How to use Galaxy for data analysis

name: Hands-on: Getting your hands-on earth data
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using the Galaxy platform. It provides hands-on experience with relevant tools and workflows. Participants will learn to manipulate and interpret environmental datasets, gaining practical skills applicable to climate science research. The tutorial covers data acquisition, processing, and visualization techniques within the Galaxy environment.
teaches: Accessing Earth System and climate data
Analyzing Earth System and climate data
Visualizing Earth System and climate data
Using the Galaxy platform for data analysis

name: Introduction to climate data
description: This tutorial teaches how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. It defines climate as the average weather condition over a 30-year period, distinguishing it from short-term weather. The material covers introductory concepts and recommends familiarity with basic Galaxy analyses.
teaches: Understanding the difference between weather and climate

name: Slides: Introduction to climate data
description: This tutorial introduces climate data analysis. It defines climate as the average state of weather conditions over a 30-year period, referencing a Wikipedia definition. It emphasizes assessing patterns of variation in meteorological variables like temperature and humidity. The slides cover the basic definition of climate and initiate a discussion on the appropriate time period for its measurement.

name: Hands-on: Sentinel 5P data visualisation
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. It focuses on working with Sentinel 5P data. This hands-on tutorial will guide you through the process of importing, processing, and visualizing Sentinel-5P data within the Galaxy platform, providing practical experience with real-world climate datasets.
teaches: Analyzing Earth System data using Galaxy
Data import and processing in Galaxy

name: Hands-on: Sentinel 5P data visualisation
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy, specifically focusing on Sentinel-5P data. It's a hands-on guide for working with this type of environmental data. The tutorial covers data access, processing workflows, and creating meaningful visualizations to explore climate patterns. Participants will gain practical skills in using Galaxy for environmental data analysis and interpretation.
teaches: Analyzing Earth System data with Galaxy

name: Hands-on: Sentinel 5P data visualisation
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using the Galaxy platform, specifically focusing on Sentinel 5P data. It’s a hands-on guide for working with this type of environmental data.  The tutorial provides practical experience with data processing workflows relevant to atmospheric composition monitoring. Users will learn techniques for data import, filtering, and visualization within the Galaxy environment. This resource is suitable for researchers and students interested in environmental data science.
teaches: Analyzing Earth System and climate data using Galaxy
Data processing workflows for atmospheric composition

name: Visualize Climate data with Panoply netCDF viewer
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using the Panoply netCDF viewer within the Galaxy environment. It provides hands-on experience with a key tool for climate data exploration.

name: Climate / Tutorial List
description: This page lists tutorials related to climate data analysis using Galaxy. It aims to teach users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data within the Galaxy platform. The page provides links to translated versions of these tutorials. It’s a landing page for climate-focused training resources.

name: Hands-on: Analyse Argo data
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. It provides a hands-on experience with Argo data. Participants will learn to perform data analysis and visualization techniques relevant to climate research.
teaches: Performing data analysis workflows for Earth System data

name: Climate / Tutorial List
description: Learn to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. This tutorial likely covers methods for working with Argo Ocean Data. These tutorials provide a hands-on introduction to climate data analysis workflows within the Galaxy platform. Participants will gain practical skills in data manipulation and visualization techniques relevant to climate research.
teaches: Accessing Earth System and climate data

name: Workflows / Analyse Argo data / Climate
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. It focuses on Argo data and workflows within the Galaxy platform. Participants will learn practical skills in data manipulation and analysis relevant to climate science.
teaches: Analyzing Earth System data with Galaxy

name: Hands-on: Collaboration with JupyterGIS / Climate
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy, specifically focusing on collaborative workflows with JupyterGIS. It provides a practical, hands-on experience for leveraging these tools in research. The tutorial covers data access, analysis, and visualization techniques. It is designed to help users work effectively with JupyterGIS within a Galaxy environment.
teaches: Accessing Earth System and climate data in Galaxy

name: Hands-on: Collaboration with JupyterGIS
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. It focuses on collaboration aspects within the JupyterGIS environment. Participants will learn practical skills for working with geospatial data in a collaborative setting. The tutorial provides a hands-on experience with data analysis and visualization techniques relevant to climate science.
teaches: Accessing and analyzing Earth System and climate data with JupyterGIS
Visualizing geospatial data for climate science
Utilizing Galaxy for climate data processing

name: Workflows / Functionally Assembled Terrestrial Ecosystem Simulator (FATES) / Climate
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy, specifically focusing on FATES workflows. It's part of the Galaxy Training Network’s curriculum. Participants will learn practical skills in data manipulation and interpretation within a climate modeling context. The tutorial assumes some familiarity with command-line interfaces and basic data analysis concepts.  It provides a hands-on experience with real-world datasets and workflows.
teaches: Accessing Earth System data with Galaxy
Analyzing climate data with FATES workflows
Visualizing Earth System and climate data
Implementing workflows for climate data analysis

name: Climate / Tutorial List
description: Provides access to Earth System and climate data analysis tutorials using Galaxy. This page serves as a list of tutorials within the climate topic. The tutorials aim to teach users how to access, analyze, and visualize climate data.  The material is part of the Galaxy Training Network and utilizes the FATES model.

name: Hands-on: Nitrate DMQC for autonomous platforms such as Argo floats
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy, focusing on Nitrate DMQC for autonomous platforms like Argo floats. It’s a hands-on tutorial designed to provide practical skills in ocean data quality control.
teaches: Accessing Earth System and climate data using Galaxy
Analyzing Nitrate DMQC data for autonomous platforms
Visualizing Earth System and climate data
Performing Data Quality Control for oceanographic measurements

name: Hands-on: Nitrate DMQC for autonomous platforms such as Argo floats
description: This tutorial provides hands-on experience with Nitrate Data Quality Control (DMQC) for data collected by autonomous platforms such as Argo floats. Participants will learn to access, analyze, and visualize this important climate data using the Galaxy platform. The tutorial focuses on practical workflows for quality control and data interpretation.
teaches: Performing data quality control on Nitrate DMQC data from Argo floats
Analyzing Earth System and climate data using Galaxy
Visualizing climate data and interpreting results

name: Hands-on: Ocean's variables study
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. It focuses on ocean variables. The tutorial is part of the Climate topic within the Galaxy Training Network.
teaches: Accessing Earth System and climate data
Visualizing Earth System and climate data using Galaxy

name: Workflows / Ocean's variables study / Climate
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using the Galaxy platform. It specifically focuses on ocean variable studies, providing workflows for accomplishing these tasks. It’s part of the broader Climate topic training, aimed at intermediate users.
teaches: Creating workflows for climate data analysis

name: Climate / Tutorial List
description: Learn to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. This tutorial list provides resources for working with climate data within the Galaxy platform, covering data access, analysis techniques, and visualization methods.
teaches: Analyzing Earth System data with Galaxy

name: Pangeo ecosystem 101 for everyone - Introduction to Xarray Galaxy Tools workflows
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy, specifically focusing on Xarray workflows. It provides instructions for working with the Pangeo ecosystem and using Galaxy tools for data manipulation. The tutorial is geared towards users new to Pangeo and Xarray within the Galaxy environment.
teaches: Accessing Earth System and climate data with Galaxy
Analyzing Earth System and climate data using Xarray workflows
Visualizing Earth System and climate data using Galaxy tools
Building scientific workflows with Galaxy and Xarray

name: Hands-on: Functionally Assembled Terrestrial Ecosystem Simulator (FATES) with Galaxy Climate JupyterLab
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. It focuses on the FATES model within the Galaxy Climate JupyterLab environment. Participants will learn to perform data analysis and visualization tasks related to terrestrial ecosystems.
teaches: Using FATES model within Galaxy Climate JupyterLab
Performing data analysis of terrestrial ecosystems

name: Climate / Tutorial List
description: This collection of tutorials provides training on accessing, analyzing, and visualizing Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. Tutorials cover a range of tools and workflows, including using FATES within a JupyterLab environment.  It aims to equip users with the skills to explore and interpret climate datasets effectively. This resource is part of the Galaxy Training Network's extensive training materials.
teaches: Accessing Earth System and climate data using Galaxy
Analyzing climate data with various tools
Visualizing climate data and interpreting results

name: Hands-on: Pangeo Notebook in Galaxy - Introduction to Xarray / Climate
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy, specifically with Pangeo and Xarray. It's a hands-on guide for data manipulation and analysis within the Galaxy platform.  Participants will learn to work with netCDF files and apply common Xarray operations. The tutorial covers data exploration and visualization techniques applicable to climate research.
teaches: Accessing Earth System data with Pangeo
Analyzing climate data using Xarray within Galaxy
Visualizing Earth System data using Galaxy
Working with netCDF files in Xarray

name: Climate / Tutorial List
description: This page lists tutorials focused on climate data analysis using the Galaxy platform. Learn to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data. Tutorials cover a range of topics relevant to climate science and data handling. The Galaxy Training Network provides resources for various skill levels.
teaches: Galaxy workflow development for climate science

name: GTN Videos
description: These are video slides for a Galaxy Training Network (GTN) tutorial. The page provides links to view the transcript, edit the source slide, view slides, and view plain text. It also links to other GTN videos.
teaches: Using Galaxy with Pangeo Notebooks for climate data analysis

name: Hands-on: Pangeo Notebook in Galaxy - Introduction to Xarray / Climate
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy and the Pangeo ecosystem, specifically focusing on Xarray. It is a hands-on learning experience. The tutorial covers the fundamentals of Xarray, a powerful Python library for working with labelled, multi-dimensional arrays, within the Galaxy interface.  Participants will learn to load, manipulate, and visualize climate data effectively.
teaches: Accessing Earth System data with Xarray
Analyzing Earth System data using Xarray within Galaxy
Visualizing Earth System data in Galaxy

name: Workflows / Pangeo Notebook in Galaxy - Introduction to Xarray / Climate
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy, specifically focusing on Xarray workflows within a Pangeo Notebook environment. It provides a practical introduction to working with climate data in Galaxy.  The tutorial covers accessing data, performing analysis using Xarray, and visualizing results. It's geared towards users new to Pangeo and Xarray within the Galaxy framework.
teaches: Accessing Earth System and climate data in Galaxy
Analyzing climate data using Xarray workflows
Visualizing Earth System data with Pangeo Notebook in Galaxy

name: Climate / Tutorial List
description: Learn to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. This tutorial likely covers data handling and analysis techniques within the Galaxy environment, specifically focused on climate data. The materials provide a pathway to understanding and applying these techniques to real-world climate challenges. This resource aims to equip users with the skills necessary to work with complex climate datasets.
teaches: Data handling techniques for climate science

name: Introduction to Galaxy and Sequence analysis
description: This learning path aims to teach you the basics of Galaxy and analysis of sequencing data. You will learn how to use Galaxy for analysis, and will be guided through the most common first steps of any genome analysis; quality control and a mapping or assembly of your genomic sequences.
teaches: Using Galaxy for sequence data analysis
Performing quality control on genomic sequences

name: Learning Pathways
description: This learning pathway provides a curated set of tutorials. It guides users through various topics with the aim of building knowledge progressively.  The tutorials are designed to be accessible and practical. This resource aims to provide a comprehensive learning experience.

name: Epigenetics data analysis with Galaxy
description: Learning pathways are sets of tutorials curated by community experts to form a coherent set of lessons around a topic, building up knowledge as you go. The pathway focuses on epigenetics data analysis using Galaxy, with tools like MACS2, bedtools and deepTools. Tutorials are recommended to be followed in the listed order.
teaches: Use of Galaxy tools for genomic analysis

name: Learning Pathway: Introduction to Galaxy and Single Cell RNA Sequence analysis
description: This learning path aims to teach you the basics of Galaxy and analysis of Single Cell RNA-seq data. You will learn how to use Galaxy for analysis, and an important Galaxy feature for iterative single cell analysis. You’ll be guided through the general theory of single analysis and then perform a basic analysis of 10X chromium data. For support throughout these tutorials, join our Galaxy single cell chat group on Matrix to ask questions!
teaches: Basic single cell RNA-seq analysis of 10X Chromium data

name: Cell RNA Sequence analysis
description: This learning path consists of curated tutorials from community experts, forming a coherent set of lessons on Cell RNA-seq analysis. It’s designed to build knowledge progressively, and following the tutorials in order is recommended.

name: Learning Pathway: Detection of AMR genes in bacterial genomes
description: This learning path aims to teach you the basic steps to detect and check Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) genes in bacterial genomes using Galaxy. It's a practical guide for learning how to perform this analysis.

name: Learning Pathway: Applying single-cell RNA-seq analysis
description: Gone is the pre-annotated, high quality tutorial data - now you have real, messy data to deal with. You have decisions to make and parameters to decide. This learning pathway challenges you to replicate a published analysis as if this were your own dataset. You will be introduced to a few more tools available for scRNA-seq in Galaxy. Finally, if our tool offerings are not enough for you, you will be directed towards how to use coding notebooks within Galaxy, setting you up to analyse scRNA-seq in R or python notebooks.  The data is messy. The decisions are tough. The interpretation is meaningful. Come here to advance your single cell skills! Note that you get two options for inferring trajectories.  For support throughout these tutorials, join our Galaxy [single cell chat group on Matrix](https://matrix.to/#/#Galaxy-Training-Network_galaxy-single-cell:gitter.im) to ask questions!
teaches: Using coding notebooks for scRNA-seq analysis

name: single-cell RNA-seq analysis
description: This learning pathway is a curated collection of tutorials designed to build knowledge around single-cell RNA-seq analysis. It encourages replicating a published analysis using Galaxy. Tutorials are presented in a specific order to facilitate learning.
teaches: Perform single-cell RNA-seq analysis using Galaxy
Understand the principles of single-cell RNA-seq data analysis

name: Learning Pathway: From Zero To Hero with Python
description: This training pathway is designed to equip individuals with foundational Python coding skills, targeting job seekers, SME employers, and those interested in life sciences. The curriculum is self-paced, uses Galaxy as a programming environment, and incorporates learning materials from The Carpentries. It is offered by the BioNT consortium.
teaches: Programming in a life sciences context

name: Learning Pathways
description: This learning pathway consists of curated tutorials by community experts. It provides a coherent set of lessons, building knowledge progressively. The pathway recommends following tutorials in the order presented. It is maintained by the Galaxy Training Network.

name: Bioinformatics Projects: Using deconvolution to get new insights from old bulk RNA-seq data
description: This learning pathway provides project ideas for educators/students to apply deconvolution techniques to existing bulk RNA-seq data. Users will need to formulate their own research questions and interpret results. Familiarity with Galaxy and single-cell RNA-seq analysis is recommended, with links to introductory pathways provided. The pathway focuses on methods, leaving research question development to the user.
teaches: Apply deconvolution techniques to bulk RNA-seq data
Formulate research questions for bioinformatics projects
Interpret results from RNA-seq data analysis

name: Finding old bulk RNA-seq data and transforming it into new results
description: This learning pathway provides a curated set of tutorials designed to build knowledge around a specific topic. It guides users through the skills needed to locate older RNA-seq datasets and repurpose them for current analysis. The pathway emphasizes following tutorials in a specific order for a coherent learning experience.
teaches: Locating and accessing older RNA-seq datasets
Transforming RNA-seq data for modern analysis
Repurposing existing data for new research questions

name: Learning Pathway: FAIR Data Management
description: This training pathway teaches how to organize, describe, and store research data according to the FAIR principles (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable). It provides guidance on implementing these principles in research workflows. The pathway is designed for researchers, data managers, and anyone involved in the research data lifecycle.  It covers practical aspects of applying FAIR principles using common tools and technologies.
teaches: Organize research data according to FAIR principles
Describe research data according to FAIR principles
Store research data according to FAIR principles
Implement FAIR principles in research workflows

name: Learning Pathway: Genome annotation for eukaryotes
description: This learning pathway teaches users how to annotate eukaryotic genomes. It covers identifying repeated regions, locating genes, determining their function, and setting up a manual curation environment with Apollo.
teaches: Identification of repeated regions in genomes

name: Learning Pathway: Genome annotation for prokaryotes
description: This learning pathway teaches how to annotate prokaryotic genome sequences, including gene positioning and function determination. It also covers setting up a manual curation environment using Apollo. The pathway is designed for intermediate to advanced users seeking comprehensive knowledge in prokaryotic genome analysis.

name: Learning Pathway: Introduction to Galaxy and Ecological data analysis
description: This learning path aims to teach you the basics of Galaxy and analysis of ecological data. You will learn how to use Galaxy for analysis, and will be guided through the common steps of biodiversity data analysis: download, check, filter and explore biodiversity data and analyze abundance data through modeling.

name: Introduction to Galaxy and Ecological data analysis
description: This learning path consists of a curated set of tutorials designed to build knowledge around a topic. The tutorials are designed to be followed in a specific order. It aims to teach the basics of Galaxy and analysis of ecological data.

name: From R to Machine Learning; an introductory course
description: This learning path aims to teach you the basics of Machine Learning using R. Initially you will learn how to code in R within the Galaxy platform, gaining some familiarity into how to wrangle and analyze data. Then, you will be guided through the various types of Machine Learning techniques, developing some simple models using R.
teaches: Data wrangling and analysis using R

name: Learning Pathway: Machine learning
description: This learning path teaches machine learning from simple concepts to complex ones. You start with a basic introduction to Machine learning. Then you move to different aspects of supervised machine learning such as classification and regression. Further topics introduce you to deep learning concepts such as convolutional and recurrent neural networks and conclude with fine-tuning a large language model trained on protein sequences.
teaches: Supervised learning techniques including classification and regression
Deep learning concepts such as convolutional and recurrent neural networks
Fine-tuning large language models for protein sequences

name: Learning Pathway: Building and Annotating Metagenome-Assembled Genomes (MAGs) from Metagenomics Reads
description: This learning path will guide you through the process of constructing and analyzing Metagenome-Assembled Genomes (MAGs) using the Galaxy platform. You will explore the key steps involved in transforming raw metagenomic data into high-quality MAGs, from preprocessing to functional annotation.  By the end of this path, you will be able to: List and describe the essential steps in MAGs construction, including quality control, assembly, binning, and refinement. Define core concepts such as MAGs, binning, and functional annotation, and understand their significance in metagenomic analysis. Explain the importance of preprocessing metagenomic reads, focusing on quality control and contamination removal. Compare the quality of MAGs using metrics like completeness and contamination, and assess their suitability for downstream analysis. Evaluate the reliability of taxonomic assignments and functional annotations by leveraging reference databases.
teaches: List and describe the essential steps in MAGs construction
Define core concepts such as MAGs, binning, and functional annotation
Explain the importance of preprocessing metagenomic reads
Compare the quality of MAGs using metrics like completeness and contamination
Evaluate the reliability of taxonomic assignments and functional annotations

name: Learning Pathway: Metagenomics data processing and analysis for microbiome
description: This learning path aims to teach the basics of Galaxy and analysis of metagenomics data. You will learn how to use Galaxy for analysis, and will be guided through the common steps of microbiome data analysis: quality control, taxonomic profiling, taxonomic binning, assembly, functional profiling, and also some applications.

name: Microbiome data analysis with Galaxy
description: This learning path provides a curated set of tutorials to learn Galaxy and microbiome data analysis. It covers key steps like quality control, taxonomic profiling, binning, assembly, and functional profiling, as well as applications of these analyses. The pathway is designed to build knowledge sequentially.

name: Learning Pathway: Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in Life Sciences using Python
description: This learning pathway covers the application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) to life sciences, particularly in the context of large omics datasets. It addresses the limitations of classical statistics when analyzing complex, high-volume data, and highlights the increasing importance of AI/ML for organizations like ELIXIR. It aims to provide training in this rapidly evolving field.
teaches: Application of AI/ML in Life Sciences
Analysis of Omics Data with Python

name: Neoantigen discovery using the iPepGen pipeline
description: This learning path introduces a comprehensive immunopeptidogenomics (iPepGen) workflow for neoantigen discovery using label-free mass spectrometry data. The modules guide you through fusion and variant database generation, peptide identification with FragPipe, peptide validation using PepQuery2, and immunogenicity assessment through HLA binding predictions and IEDB screening.
teaches: Immunogenicity assessment using HLA binding predictions

name: Learning Pathway: Proteogenomics
description: This learning path aims to teach you the basics of how to perform proteogenomics analysis of Mass spectrometry data within the Galaxy platform. You will learn how to use Galaxy for analysis and will be guided through the most common first steps of any proteogenomics database generation, to searching the database, followed by novel peptide data analysis.

name: Learning Pathway: Introductory Python
description: This is an introductory course of Python, as it was taught in Avans Hogeschool in the Netherlands. It provides foundational knowledge for those new to the Python programming language.  The pathway covers fundamental programming concepts and practical Python skills. It is designed for individuals with little to no prior programming experience.  Participants will learn to write and execute Python code, work with data structures, and develop simple applications.

name: Applying single-cell RNA-seq analysis in Coding Environments
description: Gone is the pre-annotated, high quality tutorial data - now you have real, messy data to deal with. You have decisions to make and parameters to decide. This learning pathway challenges you to replicate a published analysis as if this were your own dataset. You will perform this analysis in coding environments hosted on Galaxy, instead of Galaxy's button-based tool interface.  The data is messy. The decisions are tough. The interpretation is meaningful. Come here to advance your single cell skills! Note that you get two options: performing the analysis predominantly in R or in Python.  For support throughout these tutorials, join our Galaxy [single cell chat group on Matrix](https://matrix.to/#/#Galaxy-Training-Network_galaxy-single-cell:gitter.im) to ask questions!

name: Learning Pathway: Introductory SQL
description: This is an introductory course of SQL, as it was taught at Avans Hogeschool in the Netherlands. It provides foundational knowledge for working with SQL databases. The pathway covers basic SQL syntax, querying, and database management concepts. It's designed for individuals with little to no prior SQL experience, aiming to equip them with the skills to interact with and analyze data effectively. This resource will enable participants to perform fundamental database operations.

name: Learning Pathways
description: Learning pathways are sets of tutorials curated by community experts to form a coherent set of lessons around a topic, building up knowledge as you go. These pathways are designed to guide learners through a structured curriculum. Following the tutorials in the order they are listed is highly recommended for optimal learning. The Galaxy Training Network provides these resources to support data science skills development.

name: Onboarding for new contributors
description: This learning path aims to teach you how to contribute to GTN training materials. From editing typos to rendering test GTN sites, you will progress from being a user to a contributor. For support throughout these tutorials, join our Galaxy training network on Matrix to ask questions!
teaches: How to contribute to Galaxy Training Network materials

name: Learning Pathways
description: Learning pathways are sets of tutorials curated by community experts to form a coherent set of lessons around a topic, building up knowledge as you go. We recommend following the tutorials in the order they are listed in the pathway. These pathways provide structured learning experiences for various skill levels.

name: Learning Pathway: Tool development for a nice & shiny subdomain
description: This learning pathway guides users through building tools for Galaxy, covering Planemo for batch tools and interactive tool development. It focuses on creating, testing, and submitting tools within the Galaxy ecosystem. Users will learn how to create a subdomain and enrich it with their own tools.
teaches: Building tools for Galaxy using Planemo
Developing interactive tools within the Galaxy ecosystem
Creating and managing a Galaxy subdomain
Testing and submitting tools to Galaxy

name: Creation of a Community CoDex and lab (and optionally of a Special Interest Group (SIG))
description: This learning pathway covers the creation and maintenance of Galaxy Community CoDexes – centralized repositories for versioning and documenting community components. These codexes involve GitHub repositories with curated resources (tools, workflows, tutorials) and Community Labs for aggregating and launching these resources. The pathway explains how to set these up for a community, allowing members to share and utilize data analysis capacity without requiring extensive programming knowledge.
teaches: Creating and maintaining Galaxy Community CoDexes
Setting up GitHub repositories for community resources
Utilizing Community Labs for data analysis

name: Hands-on: Getting your hands-on earth data
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using the Galaxy platform. It focuses on practical application and hands-on experience with relevant datasets and tools. The tutorial provides a guided, hands-on experience suitable for researchers and students alike. It covers essential workflows for processing and interpreting climate-related Earth observations. Participants will gain practical skills in data manipulation and visualization within the Galaxy environment.
teaches: Using the Galaxy platform for climate data analysis

name: Hands-on: Ocean's variables study
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. It focuses on ocean variables, providing hands-on experience with relevant datasets and workflows. The training is designed for those interested in climate data analysis. Participants will learn to perform common data manipulation and visualization tasks. This tutorial is part of the Climate topic within the Galaxy Training Material.
teaches: Accessing Earth System and climate data

name: Hands-on: Ocean Data View (ODV)
description: Learn to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. This tutorial focuses on using the Ocean Data View (ODV) tool within the Galaxy environment to explore and understand oceanographic data. Participants will gain practical experience in data handling and visualization techniques.
teaches: Accessing Earth System data with ODV
Visualizing climate data with Ocean Data View
Data manipulation and visualization in a Galaxy workflow

name: Hands-on: QGIS Web Feature Services / QGIS Web Feature Services / Ecology
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, manage, and analyze ecological data through Galaxy, specifically using QGIS Web Feature Services. It provides practical experience with these tools for ecological data handling. The tutorial covers accessing and utilizing Web Feature Services within the QGIS environment for ecological analyses.
teaches: Accessing ecological data using QGIS Web Feature Services
Utilizing Web Feature Services in QGIS

name: Hands-on: Obis marine indicators
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, manage, and analyze ecological data using the Galaxy platform. It focuses on working with OBIS (Ocean Biogeographic Information System) marine indicators.  Participants will learn practical skills for data manipulation and interpretation within a marine context. The tutorial assumes some familiarity with basic ecological concepts and the Galaxy interface. It is ideal for researchers and students interested in marine data science.

name: Access data | CMEMS
description: This page provides information on how to access data from the Copernicus Marine Service. It's a central hub for data access, linking to different datasets and methods. It covers various data formats and tools, and offers guidance on data discovery and retrieval. The page also offers links to register for data access, ensuring users can fully utilize the available marine data resources.
teaches: Accessing data from the Copernicus Marine Service
Registering for data access to CMEMS resources

name: Hands-on: From NDVI data with OpenEO to time series visualisation with Holoviews
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, manage, and analyze ecological data using Galaxy, specifically focusing on NDVI data with OpenEO and visualizing time series data with Holoviews. It provides a hands-on approach to ecological data analysis.
teaches: Visualizing time series data with Holoviews

name: Galaxy - Copernicus Climate Data Store Integration
description: Galaxy is a community-driven, web-based platform for life science research. This tool provides access to the Copernicus Climate Data Store (CDS) within Galaxy, enabling researchers to analyze climate data. It's designed as an interactive notebook-style interface for seamless integration into existing workflows. Researchers can leverage a wide range of climate datasets for their projects.
teaches: Accessing climate data from the Copernicus Climate Data Store
Performing data analysis on climate datasets within Galaxy
Integrating climate data into life science research workflows
Utilizing interactive notebooks for climate data exploration

name: Hands-on: Getting your hands-on earth data
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. It’s a hands-on experience designed for practical application of data skills. Participants will learn to manipulate real-world datasets and generate meaningful insights. The tutorial is suitable for individuals with limited prior experience in data science or Earth science.  It aims to empower users with the skills to explore and understand complex environmental datasets.
teaches: Accessing Earth System and climate data
Analyzing Earth System and climate data
Visualizing Earth System and climate data
Using Galaxy for Earth science data analysis

name: Hands-on: Obis marine indicators
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, manage, and analyze Ecological data through Galaxy. It focuses specifically on OBIS (Ocean Biodiversity Information System) marine indicators. Participants will learn practical skills in data handling and analysis relevant to marine biodiversity research. The tutorial is designed for those with some prior experience in data analysis or ecology.

name: Slides: Introduction to climate data
description: This tutorial introduces the concept of climate and how it differs from weather, defining climate as the average state of weather conditions over a 30-year period. It discusses key meteorological variables used in climate assessment (temperature, humidity, pressure, wind, precipitation). The tutorial aims to provide a foundation for understanding climate data resources.
teaches: Understand the difference between climate and weather.
Identify key meteorological variables used in climate assessment.
Define climate as the average state of weather over a 30-year period.

name: Hands-on: Visualize Climate data with Panoply netCDF viewer
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy and the Panoply netCDF viewer. It's a hands-on guide for working with climate data. Participants will learn to explore and visualize netCDF files, a common format for climate datasets, using a user-friendly interface.
teaches: Using Galaxy for climate data workflows

name: Panoply 5 netCDF, HDF and GRIB Data Viewer
description: Panoply is a cross-platform application for visualizing geo-referenced and other arrays from netCDF, HDF, and GRIB datasets. It allows users to create various plots (contour, line, map) and export them in multiple formats. The software supports remote datasets and offers features like color table customization and animation export. It requires Java 11 or later. Panoply is widely used in climate and atmospheric science for visualizing large datasets.

name: Hands-on: Visualize Climate data with Panoply netCDF viewer
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Panoply, a netCDF viewer. It provides a hands-on experience with geospatial data visualization techniques.  Users will learn to work with netCDF files and create informative visualizations for climate data analysis. The tutorial is designed for both beginners and those with some experience in data visualization.
teaches: Accessing and analyzing Earth System data

name: Hands-on: Visualize Climate data with Panoply netCDF viewer
description: This tutorial teaches users how to visualize climate data using Panoply, a netCDF viewer. It focuses on scaling data and selecting appropriate color tables for effective visualization. It's part of a broader set of materials for accessing and analyzing Earth System data with Galaxy. This tutorial is suitable for researchers and data scientists who want to improve their data visualization skills.
teaches: Selecting appropriate color tables for climate data visualization

name: Hands-on: Visualize Climate data with Panoply netCDF viewer
description: This tutorial teaches users how to visualize Earth System and climate data using the Panoply netCDF viewer within the Galaxy Training Network. It focuses on accessing, analyzing, and visualizing data, specifically using orthographic projection. It's a hands-on learning resource designed to provide practical experience with climate data visualization.
teaches: Applying orthographic projection to climate data

name: Panoply 5 netCDF, HDF and GRIB Data Viewer
description: Panoply is a cross-platform application for visualizing geo-referenced and other arrays from netCDF, HDF, GRIB, and other datasets. It allows users to create various plots (color contours, line plots, maps) and export them in multiple formats. The tutorial details its capabilities, provides download links, and offers access to documentation and color tables/map overlays. It is a valuable tool for scientists working with climate and atmospheric data. Version 5.9.2 was released on 2026-03-29.

name: Quick View Data with Panoply
description: This tutorial explains how to quickly view data using the Panoply software. It's based on the official Panoply documentation and focuses on getting users started with the tool. The tutorial likely covers basic data loading and visualization techniques within Panoply. This resource is provided by the Goddard Earth Sciences Data and Information Services Center (GES DISC).

name: Visualize Climate data with Panoply netCDF viewer
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy and the Panoply netCDF viewer. It focuses on working with NetCDF files for climate data visualization.

name: Hands-on: Visualize Climate data with Panoply netCDF viewer
description: This tutorial guides users on accessing, analyzing, and visualizing Earth System and climate data using the Panoply netCDF viewer within the Galaxy environment. It’s a practical exercise focused on visualizing climate data. It's part of the climate tutorial series, providing hands-on experience with a widely used data visualization tool.  Users will learn to effectively explore and interpret climate datasets.

name: Hands-on: Visualize Climate data with Panoply netCDF viewer
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using the Panoply netCDF viewer within the Galaxy environment. It’s a hands-on guide for working with climate data visualization. Participants will learn to load, explore, and create visualizations of netCDF files.  This resource is suitable for researchers and students interested in climate science and data analysis.
teaches: Analyzing Earth System data within Galaxy

name: Hands-on: Visualize Climate data with Panoply netCDF viewer
description: This tutorial teaches users how to visualize Earth System and climate data using the Panoply netCDF viewer within the Galaxy environment. It focuses on accessing, analyzing, and visualizing georeferenced data in netCDF format. The tutorial is part of the Climate topic within the Galaxy Training Network.

name: Hands-on: Visualize Climate data with Panoply netCDF viewer
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy and the Panoply netCDF viewer. It focuses on visualizing geo-referenced data in NetCDF format. The tutorial provides hands-on experience with these tools. Participants will learn to load, manipulate, and create compelling visualizations of climate data.
teaches: Accessing and analyzing Earth System and climate data
Visualizing geo-referenced data in NetCDF format
Using Galaxy and Panoply for data visualization

name: Hands-on: Visualize Climate data with Panoply netCDF viewer
description: This tutorial teaches users how to access, analyze, and visualize Earth System and climate data using Galaxy. It focuses on using Panoply, a netCDF viewer, for data visualization. This hands-on experience will equip you with the skills to explore and interpret complex climate datasets effectively. The tutorial covers data access, analysis workflows within Galaxy, and creating insightful visualizations using Panoply.
